item.title: Bimodal Learning from Imperfect NMR Data (BLIND): A chemistry-ignorant model for autonomous structure elucidation from NMR
item.description: Automatically inferring molecular structure from analytical measurements is a key step toward closing the loop in autonomous chemical research. Yet it remains challenging, because structurally relevant information is distributed across multiple channels, ...

item.title: Reactive Molecular Dynamics of Hydrogen Evolution at Charged Interfaces Using Machine Learning Potentials
item.description: Understanding electrochemical reaction mechanisms requires atomistic simulations that simultaneously capture charged interfaces, explicit solvent, and long-time dynamics, which remain beyond the reach of conventional ab initio molecular dynamics. Here, we ...

item.title: Effects of laser beam irradiation on displacement of microparticles at an air–wet gel interface
item.description: Optical trapping based on radiation pressure has enabled advanced manipulation of microparticles. Previous studies have primarily focused on Gaussian-beam optical trapping of nanoparticles using tightly focused laser beams. In such systems, high-intensity ...

item.title: Transition-State Analysis Reveals a Noncanonical Multi-Step Pathway for O-GlcNAc Transferase Substrate Recognition
item.description: O-GlcNAc transferase (OGT) modifies thousands of nuclear and cytoplasmic proteins, primarily within intrinsically disordered regions (IDRs), yet how it selectively recognizes such diverse substrates remains a central question in O-GlcNAc biology. The ...

item.title: Integrating ToxCast/ToxRefDB Data with Quantitative Adverse Outcome Pathway for Risk Assessment of Endocrine Disrupting Chemicals using Machine Learning
item.description: Endocrine disrupting chemicals (EDCs) pose complex, multi-mechanistic health risks that are difficult to evaluate using conventional in vitro or in vivo approaches alone. Here, we developed a quantitative adverse outcome pathway (qAOP) model integrating ...

item.title: Phonon Spectra Reveal How Selective Fluorination Enhances Hole Transport in Benzodithiophenes (BDT)
item.description: Selective fluorination is one of the most widely used chemical modifications for improving hole mobility in donor-type organic semiconductors. It is commonly assumed that the benefits arise from isolated geometric or electronic descriptors, such as a ...

item.title: Convenient Implementation of Rung-3.5 Density Functionals in Standard Gaussian-Basis Codes
item.description: Developing more broadly accurate density functional approximations can be accomplished not only by new parameterizations with existing ingredients, but also by using new ingredients encoding new physics, such as rung-3.5 energy densities that allow a ...

item.title: Deep-AGAPE: An Open-source Web-Based Machine Learning Tool for Predicting G-Quadruplex Ligand Stabilization
item.description: The identification of biologically active molecules within a vast chemical space remains a major challenge in drug discovery. In this context, G-quadruplexes (G4s), non-canonical nucleic acid structures involved in gene regulation and cancer-related ...

item.title: Metal-Organic Cages to functionalize CNTs: higher dispersibility and phase-transport
item.description: Multi-walled carbon nanotubes (MWCNTs) have potential for a range of industrial applications. However, their utility is constrained by challenges related to processing and dispersibility due to their tendency to aggregate in solvents, especially polar ...

item.title: Coherent Pulsed Dynamic Nuclear Polarization with Endogenous Metal-Ions
item.description: Dynamic nuclear polarization (DNP) can overcome the intrinsically low sensitivity of nuclear magnetic resonance (NMR) and enables structural characterization of a wide range of materials. In inorganic solids, DNP can be achieved by introducing ...

item.title: Creatine as an Organocatalyst for the Synthesis of 2,3-Dihydroquinazolin-4(1H)-one Derivatives via Multi-component Reactions at Room Temperature
item.description: Creatine's potential as an organocatalyst for organic synthesis is demonstrated specifically for the preparation of 2,3-dihydroquinazolin-4(1H)-one derivatives. Use of a multi-component sequence involving aldehydes, isatoic anhydride, and ammonium acetate ...

item.title: California’s plan to decarbonize electricity omits key greenhouse gas emissions
item.description: Regional decarbonization or climate action plans in the US generally ignore the greenhouse gas (GHG) emissions of renewable electricity generation systems that occur beyond the operational stage. Our analysis of California’s prospective electricity sector ...

item.title: A Cross-Species Comparison of Excitation Energy Transfer in Red-Lineage Photosystem I
item.description: The Photosystem I supercomplex (PSI) is a central component of photosynthesis in plants and algae. Its antenna subunits absorb the light energy and transfer it through an interconnected pigment network to the reaction center (RC), where it is stored as ...

item.title: The Human ADME Score: A Quantitative Metric of Drug-likeness for Orally Administered Compounds
item.description: Quantitative assessment of ADME-related drug-likeness remains challenging as small-molecule drug discovery expands into larger and more complex chemical space. We hypothesized that drug-likeness for orally administered compounds is best quantified at the ...

item.title: A physical aid to site-selective modification of a peptide: targeting of single residues for conjugation, stapling, and labeling with a nanopore and mechanical positioning
item.description: A physical device to aid post-synthetic modification of specific single residues in a peptide at the single molecule level is proposed; it is similar to a structure that was recently proposed for peptide synthesis without side-chain protection (doi: ...

item.title: NovoExpert-2: State-of-the-Art ADMET Prediction via Gradient-Boosted Trees on MapLight Fingerprints and GIN Embeddings
item.description: We present NovoExpert-2, a dual-method approach to molecular property prediction that achieves state-of-the-art performance on five endpoints of the Therapeutics Data Commons (TDC) ADMET benchmark. Our primary model combines MapLight-style fingerprint fea-...

item.title: A Density Matrix Theory for Photothermal Two-Dimensional Infrared Spectroscopy
item.description: Two-dimensional infrared (2D-IR) spectroscopy can resolve vibrational anharmonicity, mode coupling, and energy transfer. The conventional 2D-IR detects nonlinear polarization that generates propagating photons after sample’s light matter interaction with ...

item.title: ReactiveLJ: A Coordination-Dependent Lennard-Jones-Based Potential for Reversible Bonding in Coarse-Grained Simulations
item.description: Associative polymer networks are cross-linked by reversible bonds whose continual formation, dissociation, and exchange rearrange network topology and thereby govern viscoelastic and transport properties. Coarse-grained (CG) molecular simulations offer a ...

item.title: Photoactivated chlorine dioxide oxygenation of freshly cleaved HOPG surfaces

item.title: Mind the Driver: Behavior-aware Battery Usage Prediction to Reduce Electric Vehicle Range Anxiety
item.description: Range anxiety remains a major concern for electric vehicle (EV) drivers due to unpredictable charge usage influenced by terrain and user behavior variations. To address this issue, we propose a data-driven approach to provide accurate trip-specific ...

item.title: Multiplexed Imaging through Affinity Hierarchies in a Supramolecular Host
item.description: Bioorthogonal chemistry has transformed molecular imaging by enabling highly selective synthetic labeling of biomolecules in their native habitats. However, multiplexed imaging remains fundamentally constrained by the limited number of mutually orthogonal ...

item.title: Sb-Doped SrSnO3: a lead free perovskite oxide for sustainable photoresponsive optoelectronics

item.title: Solution-state Magnetic Bistability in Cobalt Corrole Complexes with Switchable Sidearms

item.description: Recently, a new category of machine learning approaches for tabular data has emerged: tabular foundation models (TFM), based on in-context learning. A TFM is a neural network (usually a transformer) pre-trained primarily on synthetic data. Its input is an ...

item.title: Cation competition and low pH protect protocell membranes from divalent cation disruption

item.title: Reinforcement of Polyvinyl Chloride Using Plasma-Activated Aramid Fibers and Surface-Bonded Compatibilizer

On the Causes of Chemical Structure: Bonds, Valence, and Molecular Geometry as Boundary-Thickness Minimisation Among Individuated Atoms
item.description: We ask why there are chemical structures at allwhy atoms compose into molecules of definite stoichiometry and shape rather than remaining sep-arate or merging without limit—and answer it from a single hypothesis already known to force atomic structure: ...

item.title: Beyond the Spectral Shift: First-Principles Colorimetric Screening of Donor-Acceptor Copolymers as pH-Responsive Chromatic Indicators
item.description: Color change upon change of pH is a key indicator for smart packaging that signals food spoilage. Donor-acceptor (D–A) conjugated copolymers are attractive candidates for such indicators, as their charge-transfer transitions are sensitive to protonation. ...

item.title: Scalable and Generalizable Analog Design via Learning Medicinal Chemistry Intuition from Matched Molecular Pair Transformations
item.description: Chemical analog design in the hit-to-lead and lead optimization stages of drug discovery relies on systematic structural modifications often guided by medicinal chemistry intuition. Although a matched molecular pair (MMP) provides an interpretable ...

item.title: The Kihara Potential of Convex Bodies of Different Shapes: A Comprehensive Assessment
item.description: A comprehensive study of the Kihara intermolecular potential of convex bodies is reported. Molecular cores of specific shapes, including globular, tetrahedral, octahedral, spherocylindrical, triangular, rectangular, hexagonal and circular, are employed to ...

item.title: Scalable Low-Energy Molecular Conformer Generation with Quantum Mechanical Accuracy
item.description: Understanding molecular geometry is crucial for biological activity and chemical reactivity; however, computational methods for generating 3D structures are limited by the vast scale of conformational space and the complexities of stereochemistry. We ...

item.title: A first-principles magnetostructural coefficient for the [2Fe–2S] cluster of Anabaena ferredoxin
item.description: Iron–sulfur proteins mediate electron transfer throughout biology, yet the quantitative relationship between atomic-scale structural fluctuations and magnetic exchange within their [2Fe–2S] cofactors remain unestablished for any biological system. Here, ...

item.title: Mo(CO)₃(cht) as an air-stable precatalyst for highly Z-selective one-bond isomerization of terminal alkenes
item.description: ABSTRACT: The selective transposition of terminal alkenes toZ-2-alkenes remains a formidable challenge. Here, we report that the air-stable molybdenum(0) complex2(Mo(CO)3(cht)) serves as a highly efficient precatalyst when combined in a 1:1:1 ratio ...

item.title: Colossal negative thermal expansion in a coordination polymer glass
item.description: Negative thermal expansion (NTE), an anomalous thermal behaviour of contraction upon temperature increase, is extensively studied in crystalline solids. However, inducing NTE in glassy states in amorphous solids has been challenging, and the coefficient ...

item.title: Geometry-Dependent Redistribution of Exocytotic Spike-Shape Populations Revealed by Ultrafast Disk and Nanotip Acetylcholine Biosensors
item.description: Measurements of sub-millisecond acetylcholine (ACh) exocytosis have only recently become possible through the development of ultrafast electrochemical ACh biosensors. These biosensors convert transient presynaptic ACh release events into sub-millisecond ...

item.title: Photorelease of Anandamide at the Cell Plasma Membrane with Optically-Cleavable Targeted Ligands
item.description: Anandamide (AEA) was the first endocannabinoid discovered to act on CB1 cannabinoid receptors, which are the primary target of the psychoactive compounds found in marijuana. AEA and cannabinoid signaling are ubiquitous throughout the body and are ...

item.title: Hydronium-Driven Concerted Ring-Opening: Deciphering the Structural Collapse of 2,2,6,6-Tetramethylpiperidine-1-oxyl (TEMPO) in Aqueous Flow Batteries
Dual cancer-selective lysosomally-targeted antibody–drug conjugates expand the ADC-druggable proteome
item.description: Antibody–drug conjugates (ADCs) are standard-of-care therapies for many types of cancer, with an expanding representation in clinical development pipelines.1 A key determinant of efficacy for most ADCs is their ability to internalize into the target cell ...

item.title: QuantumPioneer: Scalable generation of quantum chemical data for solution-phase hydrogen transfer reactions
item.description: High-fidelity quantum chemical (QM) datasets that jointly resolve reaction thermochemistry, kinetics, and solvation at scale remain scarce, especially for radical chemistry. We introduce QuantumPioneer, an open-access reaction-centered QM database and ...

item.title: Promoting N−N Bond Formation of Ammonia Oxidation by a Dangling Carboxylate Proton Relay
item.description: Inspired by enzymatic proton-coupled electron transfer (PCET) control, introducing proton relays into catalysts' second coordination sphere accelerates proton-transfer reactions, yet this strategy remains underexplored for N–H bond cleavage in ammonia ...

item.title: Extending the Python RESP Framework for Extra Point Charge Fitting
item.description: The Restrained Electrostatic Potential (RESP) method is widely used to derive electrostatic parameters for Newtonian-based models. However, conventional nuclei centered charge models can struggle to reproduce anisotropic features such as halogen σ-holes ...

item.title: Topology-Based Lattice Engineering in DNA Nanomaterials
item.description: In this study, we establish the topological adaptability and structural resilience of periodic soft matter entanglements using the DNA tensegrity triangle, a foundational motif in structural DNA nanotechnology, as a model system. Motivated by the ...

item.title: Insight and Optimization Derived from Principal Component Analysis of Free Energy Surfaces
item.description: Application of Principal Component Analysis (PCA) to the free energy surfaces of a family of chemical compositions participating in a common reaction mechanism is shown to provide the number of degrees of freedom over which the free energy surfaces vary ...

item.title: Breaking Kinetic Symmetry: How Wet–Dry Cycling Ratchets Prebiotic Chemistry Far from Equilibrium
item.description: Wet-dry cycling drives formation of biological oligomers from unactivated monomers, but the mechanism of directional accumulation has remained unclear. Low water activity and high monomer concentrations favor condensation; high water activity and low ...

item.title: Assembly of Membranized Coacervates through Transmembrane Peptides
Mucus Anchoring Protein (MAP) – Antibody Conjugates: A Strategy to Enhance Antiviral Efficacy
item.description: Respiratory viruses initiate infections at the airway mucosa, where improved localization and retention of neutralizing antibodies at the infection interface may enhance antiviral activity under mucus-containing conditions. To enhance antibody ...

item.title: An element-resolved coupled cluster atomization energy data set ranging across the periodic table
item.description: We introduce the total atomization energy periodic table compass (TAE-PTComp), a collection of 67 atomization energy data sets, each specifically designed to probe various chemical environments of a characteristic element of the periodic table. In total, ...

item.title: High-resolution MALDI imaging mass spectrometry of wholebody adult zebrafish
item.description: We developed an optimized sample preparation workflow tailored for high-spatial resolution matrix-assisted laser desorption/ionization imaging mass spectrometry (MALDI IMS) of adult zebrafish tissue. Zebrafish (Danio rerio) are an important model organism ...

item.title: Proteomics as an Emerging Tool for Understanding Biotransformation and Toxicological Impacts of Contaminants
item.description: Mass-spectrometry-based proteomics is rapidly advancing, becoming fast, affordable, and increasingly accessible to environmental scientists. This perspective outlines how proteomics can illuminate biotransformation processes and sublethal organism-level ...

item.title: Structure-based virtual screening identifies structurally novel, parasite-selective inhibitors of Plasmodium falciparum dihydroorotate dehydrogenase
item.description: Background. Dihydroorotate dehydrogenase (DHODH) is a clinically validated antimalarial target: Plasmodium falciparum depends on de novo pyrimidine biosynthesis and, unlike the human host, cannot salvage pyrimidines. The leading inhibitor chemotype — the ...

item.title: Systematic Expansion of Chemical Reaction Space from Graph-Based Model Reactions
item.description: Computational kinetic modeling relies on accurate transition-state geometries and activation barriers. While machine learning models trained on ab initio data have shown promise for transition-state search and barrier prediction, they often struggle to ...

item.title: OPTIMIZATION OF WASTE ENGINE OIL REGENERATION USING ACID-ACTIVATED BENTONITE
item.description: The conditions for deep acid activation of natural bentonite have been substantiated, and its effectiveness in the process of regenerating waste engine oil has been investigated. It has been established that the modification leads to a radical ...

item.title: Straightforward structure determination of beam-sensitive low-symmetry compounds at room temperature by precession SerialED
item.description: SerialED is acknowledged as an easily automatable alternative to 3DED, especially promising for the structure elucidation of very beam sensitive samples, like pharmaceuticals, porous framework materials for catalysis and macromolecules. Yet, SerialED in ...

item.title: Practical One-pot Synthesis of Cyclic Conjugated Amides from Ketones
item.description: Conjugated amides are valuable building blocks in organic synthesis; however, general access to cyclic conjugated amides remains limited, restricting their broader use in method development and complex molecule synthesis. Herein, we report a practical one-...

item.title: Biophysical Descriptors of Single Protein Domains
item.description: Quantitative relationships among geometric and surface-chemical descriptors of single-domain proteins remain insufficiently characterized, despite their relevance for interpreting structural datasets and modeling biomolecular interactions. Using a curated,...

item.title: Deciphering the anion dynamics in the fully deuterated pyridinium-based ionic liquid [C5Py][NTf2] via 19F fast field cycling NMR relaxometry and MD simulation
item.description: Fast Field Cycling (FFC) NMR relaxometry allows the investigation of molecular dynamics over a broad frequency range from 10 kHz up to 100 MHz. However, quantitative data interpretation is often limited by system-specific motional models and simplified ...

item.title: Genetically-targeted and red-emitting fluorescent indicators for subcellular zinc imaging
item.description: The concentration of biologically relevant metal cations can be monitored with subcellular precision thanks to chemogenetic fluorescent indicators that combine a small molecular probe with a recombinant protein tag but, despite a few reported examples ...

item.title: Why a Single Mutation Dims the Light: Local Chromophore Organization Modulates Fluorescence in smURFP
item.description: Predicting mutation-induced photophysical changes in bilin-binding fluorescent proteins remains challenging. Here, we investigate the origin of fluorescence modulation in smURFP and its lowfluorescent-quantum-yield Y56R variant using classical MD ...

item.title: Data-Efficient and Fast Machine Learning Molecular Dynamics through Integrated Active Learning and Knowledge Distillation
item.description: We develop data-efficient machine learning interatomic potentials (MLIPs) for fast molecular dynamics simulations, combining DeePMD and MACE models within an active learning and knowledge distillation framework. Using liquid water as a case study, we ...

item.title: Distribution and Function of Pyrimidine Nucleoside 2'-Hydroxylases across the Tree of Life
item.description: Pyrimidine nucleoside metabolism is classically divided into phosphorolytic and hydrolytic pathways mediated by nucleoside phosphorylases and nucleoside hydrolases (NSHs), respectively. While ribonucleoside turnover is well characterized, it remains ...

item.title: Correlating the distribution of site energies, activation energies and saddle point energies in the energy landscape of a lithium borate glass
item.description: Long range transport of ions in a solid electrolyte is subject to the potential energy landscape in which local hopping of ions occurs. Local hopping occurs between neighboring sites (energy minima). It involves passing a local saddle point implying the ...

item.title: Designer Pseudopeptide for In Situ Liquid Crystal Gelation toward Smart Window Applications
item.description: Supramolecular self-assembly serves as a fundamental backbone for designing multifunctional gel materials. A simpler diphenylalanine (Phe-Phe) sequence has been strategically modified with distinct terminal functional groups, namely, tert-butoxycarbonyl (...

item.title: GraPAS: Gradient-Matched Probabilistic Analytical Spectroscopy Denoiser for Self-Supervised Photoacoustic Signal Recovery in Analytical Chemistry
item.description: Photoacoustic spectroscopy (PAS) is a powerful analytical chemistry technique that combines optical absorption selectivity with acoustic detection sensitivity, enabling non-destructive characterization of chemical species in complex matrices. However, the ...

item.title: Autonomous Multi-Agent Reconstruction of Prebiotic Reaction Networks Reveals Organizational Features of Amino Acid Synthesis
item.description: Decades of origin-of-life research have uncovered hundreds of prebiotically plausible reactions across hydrothermal, surface, and biochemical environments, yet this knowledge remains fragmented across chemistry, geochemistry, and astrobiology. Here we ...

item.title: One-Step Access to Ultra-Bright Macrocyclic Oligo-BODIPYs Featuring Long-Lived Cyclic Excitons
item.description: The controlled organization of chromophores into defined assemblies with collective photophysical properties is a key concept for natural and artificial photonic systems. A template-free, one-step synthesis affords a series of methylene-bridged ...

item.title: CACHE Challenge #4: Targeting the TKB Domain of the E3 ligase CBLB, an Immuno-Oncology Target
item.description: The Critical Assessments of Computational Hit-finding Experiments (CACHE) are prospective benchmarking exercises where small molecule ligands predicted for a nominated protein of interest are procured and tested experimentally. In CACHE #4, 23 ...

item.title: RESEARCH ARTICLE Fast Isotopic [18F]Fluoride Exchange in Iminosulfur Oxydifluorides and Triflimidoyl Fluorides: A novel approach for [18F]tagging of primary amine-derived precursors
item.description: Direct incorporation of fluorine-18 into primary amine- derived molecules remains a major challenge in radiochemistry. Current strategies largely rely on multistep conjugation of electrophilic prosthetic groups, limiting automation and generating ...

item.title: WHERE TO CUT, HOW DEEP: BPE AND UNIGRAM-LM ON CHEMISTRY SMILES
item.description: Every chemical language model reading SMILES begins with a tokenizer, yet the field has inherited byte-pair encoding (BPE) from natural language with little scrutiny. In natural language, BPE’s principal alternative, Unigram-LM, is known to build ...

item.title: Chalcogen-Controlled Triplet Harvesting in Benzophenone-Derived Chromophores: A Theoretical Investigation of Excited-State Relaxation Pathways
item.description: Efficient triplet-state harvesting in organic chromophores is governed by a delicate interplay between excited-state energetics, orbital character, spin–orbit coupling, and vibronic relaxation pathways. Here, we present a systematic theoretical ...

item.title: Photocatalytic and Copper-Catalyzed 1,3-Aminoarylation of Aryl Cyclopropanes: Complementary Mechanistic Strategies for Synthesizing 3,3‑Diarylpropylamines
item.description: A regioselective 1,3-aminoarylation of aryl cyclopropanes is reported through two complementary catalytic strategies based on distinct reactivity. First, photoactivation of N-chloro-N-(phenylsulfonyl)benzenesulfonamide (NCBSI) enables a rapid and ...

item.title: Three-color Photopharmacology: Chromatically Orthogonal Visible-Light Photocages for Optical Control of Adrenergic Receptors
item.description: Photopharmacology enables precise optical control of biological function through lightresponsive ligands, yet multiplexed photocontrol has been constrained by limited wavelength selectivity and reliance on UV-activated photocages. Here we report a three-...

item.title: A Nickel-Olefin Pincer Complex as Catalyst for Aldehyde Hydrogenation under Mild Conditions via a Cooperative Mechanism
item.description: The use of a Ni(0)-olefin pincer complex for the base-free catalytic hydrogenation of aldehydes under mild conditions is reported. Insights from DFT calculations complemented by isotope labeling experiments support an unusual H2 activation mechanism ...

item.title: Arginine tuning of coiled-coil peptides yields single-domain nanocrystals
item.description: Programming three-dimensional order in peptide-based materials requires lateral interactions that are precise enough to propagate over hundreds of nanometres, yet robust enough to operate in water. Here, we show that a rationally designed coiled-coil ...

item.title: Where do proteomics people come from?
item.description: The field of proteomics is one of the fastest growing scientific disciplines in the world today. Beginning in 2020, ambitious market predictions anticipated proteomics to more than double in market size in 4-6 years, and to even surpass the well-...

item.title: SEA WASTE GOLD: NOVEL HYDROPHOBIC TO HYDROPHILIC SWITCHABLE DEEP EUTECTIC SOLVENTS FOR THE SELECTIVE EXTRACTION AND RECOVER OF ASTAXANTHIN FROM BLUE CRAB SHELL
item.description: Callinectes sapidus crab, commonly known as blue crab, is an invasive species known for its easy adaptability, high reproduction rate and aggressive behaviour. Its meat is edible and widely used in food industry, so its use is directly linked to a high ...

item.title: Chemometric exploration of multispectral fluorescence landscapes for dried fruit extracts profiling
item.description: The food industry requires rapid, reliable, and cost-effective analytical methods for quality and production process control. Fluorescence spectroscopy, combined with chemometric methods, offers a promising alternative to more complex analytical ...

item.title: Programmable Porosity in Polymers via Microfluidic Templating and 3D Printing
item.description: Polymeric functionally graded porous materials (pFGPMs) enable spatial control over transport and mechanical response in a continuous solid in which porosity is strategically placed rather than uniformly distributed. Early research on the production of ...
